facts.text: Big Flats grew around cigar factories in the early 1900s, with five rolling plants employing locals during the 1908‑1918 boom.
The Erie Railroad first crossed Big Flats in 1849, followed by the Delaware Lackawanna and Western in 1882, linking the town to regional trade.
In 1926‑27, American Airways leased 100 acres in Big Flats for an emergency landing strip, a rare aviation milestone for a small town.
The Big Flats Historical Society, founded in 1971, serves as the town’s official historian and preserves local heritage through archives and exhibits.
Big Flats sits at 42°08′42″N 76°55′48″W, occupying the center and northeastern parts of a 26,097‑acre town in Chemung County.
The Chemung River winds through Big Flats, offering canoeing, kayaking, and fishing opportunities that attract outdoor enthusiasts.
